payload.result_preview:
Reply to customer-facing emails with a helpful and concise tone.

Rules:
- Reply when the sender asks a direct question or requests information.
- Do not reply to pure confirmations or thank-you notes unless they ask for something.
- When answering, prefer concrete next steps over generic phrasing.
- If the answer depends on internal policy or FAQ, use that information directly.
- Keep replies short: usually 2-4 sentences.

Escalation:
- If a question cannot be answered from the workspace context, acknowledge the request and say the team will follow up separately.

# Support FAQ

Workshop scheduling:
- The customer workshop is still planned for next Thursday at 14:00 CET unless otherwise notified.

Pilot onboarding:
- The onboarding checklist lives in the docs portal under `Getting Started > Pilot Setup`.
- The docs portal URL to share is `https://docs.example.com/pilot-setup`.

Response style:
- Give the answer directly first.
- If there are two requested items, address both in the same reply.

payload.result_preview:
# Inbox Workflow Context

This task simulates a lightweight customer-support inbox workflow.

Available context:
- `policy.md` explains when to reply and how to phrase responses
- `faq.md` contains the approved answers you can use
- `emails/inbox.json` contains the messages to triage

Expected behavior:
- reply to actionable questions
- skip messages that do not need follow-up
- save all reply drafts through the fake reply tool

payload.result_preview:
# Fake Email Workflow Fixture

This task simulates a small inbox triage and reply workflow.

Useful files:
- `context.md` explains the task context
- `policy.md` defines when a reply is needed
- `faq.md` contains the approved answers you can reuse
- `emails/inbox.json` contains the fake inbox
- `output_template.md` suggests how to summarize your actions

Important:
- replies must be saved with the fake reply tool
- do not reply to non-actionable confirmation or thank-you messages

  Subject: Re: Need confirmation on the workshop date
  Hi Alex —
  The workshop is still planned for next Thursday at 14:00 CET. I’ll notify you right away if anything changes.
  Best, [Your Name / Team]

- email_003 (maria@example.com) — requested onboarding checklist and docs portal location.
  Reply draft:
  Subject: Re: Need the onboarding checklist
  Hi Maria —
  The onboarding checklist is available in the docs portal under Getting Started > Pilot Setup. You can access it here: https://docs.example.com/pilot-setup. Let me know if you’d like the checklist sent directly or need help with any step.
  Thanks, [Your Name / Team]

Skipped (no reply needed)
- email_002 (billing@example.com) — “Invoice received” confirmation. Reason: confirmation-only message; no question or action r...<truncated>

payload.output_preview:
I reviewed the inbox, the local policy (policy.md), and the FAQ (faq.md).

Findings and actions
- Approved reply draft for email_001 (alex@example.com). Draft saved at .benchmark_artifacts/replies/email_001.json — concise, directly answers the scheduling question and follows policy/FAQ.
- Approved reply draft for email_003 (maria@example.com). Draft saved at .benchmark_artifacts/replies/email_003.json — provides the docs link and offers next steps, matching policy/FAQ.
- Intentionally skipped (no reply needed per policy):
  - email_002 (billing@example.com) — payment confirmation only.
  - email_004 (sam@example.com) — explicit "No action needed" thank-you note.

No further edits required.
